meaning: n.补缺者,权宜之计adj.暂时的,权宜的
examples: The arrangement was only intended as a stopgap.
Gone are the days when work was just a stopgap between leaving school and getting married.
Could lead to garbage distributions created as a stopgap to make money.
Regardless of who you choose to work with, remember that CSR consultants are just a stopgap.
Others have attempted a more stopgap approach, only to get the blame when services are disrupted.
